name: Kodak Portra 400
description: Known for exceptionally smooth skin tones, warm organic color palettes, and very fine grain structure.

name: Kodak Vision3 500T (Tungsten)
description: Legendary theatrical film stock with excellent shadow detail, rich blues/teals, and expressive grain.

name: Fujifilm Superia 400
description: Vibrant color palette with highlighted greens, cool shadow undertones, and active grain.

name: Ilford HP5 Plus (B&W)
description: Classic monochrome film stock featuring prominent silver halide grain and strong mid-tone contrast.

name: Eastman Double-X 5222
description: Rich, historical black and white look with moody shadows, bright highlights, and retro texture.

name: Kodachrome 64 Vintage
description: Highly saturated, contrasty slide film look with warm tones, popular in late 20th-century cinematography.

name: Pristine Digital (ArriRAW)
description: Zero grain, extreme color fidelity, ultra-clean highlights, and optimal dynamic recovery.
